title: The Adventure Begins
logline: Keen leaves home trying to look fearless, sails into a storm, and discovers that true courage is love moving through fear toward someone who needs him.
declaration: This episode is about the first time a child steps away from home and discovers that courage is not being fearless — it is carrying love with you into the unknown. When it ends, the child watching should feel, “I could be brave even if my tummy wobbles”; the parent beside them should feel the ache and pride of letting a child go while still wanting to hold on. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, out at sea and underwater, because Keen stops performing confidence and acts from love before he knows he can win. The colour of this episode is storm-washed aquamarine — morning gold darkened by fear, then lit from inside by belonging. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands glow underwater before the crystal has ever been awarded — because it trusts the child to feel that love and courage were already inside him before anyone named them.

theme.declaration: This episode is about the first time a child steps away from home and discovers that courage is not being fearless — it is carrying love with you into the unknown. When it ends, the child watching should feel, “I could be brave even if my tummy wobbles”; the parent beside them should feel the ache and pride of letting a child go while still wanting to hold on. The scene that carries the most weight is Scene 7, out at sea and underwater, because Keen stops performing confidence and acts from love before he knows he can win. The colour of this episode is storm-washed aquamarine — morning gold darkened by fear, then lit from inside by belonging. The moment I am most proud of is Keen seeing his father’s wristbands glow underwater before the crystal has ever been awarded — because it trusts the child to feel that love and courage were already inside him before anyone named them.
theme.theme: Growing up means stepping into the big world with fear still inside you, and discovering that love can travel with you as courage.
theme.leadArc: Keen wants to prove he is grown-up, capable, and brave enough to leave home; what he needs is to stop performing fearlessness and understand that real courage is love in motion, felt through trembling paws, remembered family, and the choice to help someone else even while scared.
theme.storySpine: Once upon a time, in Crystal Cove, there was a young bear named Keen who wanted to prove he was ready for the wide world. Every day, he tried to be brave by acting sure, checking everything twice, and hiding the wobble underneath. Until one day, he left home with his father’s wristbands and sailed straight into a storm. Because of that, he became lost, frightened, and dependent on a new friend, Squeaky, to find a way through. Because of that, when Squeaky was trapped, Keen stopped thinking about looking brave and chose to help, diving into the water with fear still inside him. Until finally, his courage revealed itself, the Crystal Bears welcomed him, and the Aquamarine crystal found its home in his wristbands. And ever since then, Keen has known that wherever he goes, he is not alone — and bravery begins with the heart, not the absence of fear.
theme.promise: By the final frame, the audience must feel the warm ache of a first goodbye transformed into belonging: Keen is still far from home, the sea is still big, but he has found a circle that can hold him.
theme.throughline: The tonal spine is bright adventure with a homesick bruise underneath: the comedy of Fuzzby’s chaos and Keen’s overconfident wobbling keeps the child buoyant, while the parent feels the goodbye, the father’s absence, the storm, and the truth that letting go is an act of love.
theme.selCompetency: Courage — Keen’s Aquamarine arc teaches responsible brave choice-making: true courage is not fearlessness, but acting with care while fear is present.
theme.haidt: For the parent, the episode speaks to the anxious-generation fear that children are safest when every risk is removed; beneath the adventure, it argues gently that kids need meaningful independence, embodied challenge, real-world competence, and trusted connection — not constant rescue — to discover they can cope.
theme.pressureTest: This truth is universal and earnable here because every major beat can test the gap between Keen’s performed confidence and his real need for connection: the careful packing, the goodbye, the lost boat, the dive, the wristbands, and the welcome. The danger is tipping into saccharine by making courage sound like a lesson, treating the crystal as a prize for heroism, or letting the rescue become spectacle instead of a scared child choosing love. The trap every scene must avoid is false fearlessness: if Keen ever seems simply heroic rather than frightened-and-acting-anyway, the episode loses its soul. The other trap is resolving the ache too cleanly; the ending must stay warm but not erase the cost of leaving Mum, losing the boat, or stepping into a bigger life.

beatRule: one 10-12s Seedance take per beat — the beat directs its own internal cuts (director skill v5.0)
_note: Authored by cb_director (Gate 1, BEAT-NATIVE) from the script — 44 beats, 10 scenes.
style: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film computer-generated imagery (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. 16:9. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat.

scenes.name: Rainforest Pollen Run
scenes.location: A narrow aerial pathway through the rainforest wildflower canopy, with towering pollen-heavy blossoms, broad springy leaves, and a few low crossing branches arranged as a playful obstacle corridor for fast bee flight.
scenes.time: Morning, early in the same day.
scenes.weather: Warm breeze, clear sky beginning to hold an unseen pressure far away.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden morning through canopy, soft pollen scatter, saturated friendly greens; the first distant thunder subtly cools the highlights near the end.
scenes.look: Empty establishing plate with tall open pollen-flowers dominating foreground, midground, and background, creating a clear left-to-right flight lane through the blooms. Broad green leaves jut into the corridor at soft collision height, and one thin branch crosses the midground path for the later tumble. Fine golden pollen floats in warm morning god-rays; the upper background holds a small slice of clear sky with the faintest distant greying at the edge, hinting at pressure beyond the safe play-space. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the world feeling playful and safe just before it becomes bigger than anyone expected, so the audience feels laughter with a tiny cloud forming at the edge.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest pollen corridor — warm morning tall-flower bee's-eye wide
scenes.definingFeature: a bee-scale tunnel of colossal pollen-heavy flowers with broad springy leaves intruding into the flight path
scenes.colorTemperature: warm amber morning safety with a barely cooling sky edge
scenes.cameraHeight: low bee's-eye looking slightly up through the flower corridor

scenes.name: Aida's Sanctuary
scenes.location: A quiet Crystal Cove shoreline sanctuary where gentle waves meet a small ritual space at the forest edge, with an open path leading back into the trees.
scenes.weather: Calm coastal air with distant wind beginning to gather beyond the cove.
scenes.lighting: Warm rose-gold morning, soft reflected water light, rose quartz glow blooming gently during the vision and fading into a slightly wind-cooled atmosphere.
scenes.look: An empty, tender morning plate dominated by the crystal bowl set on a smooth shoreline stone near the waterline, with the wand resting beside the rim ready for use; gentle waves roll softly across the foreground, the bowl sits in the midground as the clear focal point, and an empty forest path recedes in the background for Aida’s exit. The frame is calm, uncluttered, and expectant, with distant coastal air beginning to stir the leaves beyond the cove.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about an elder sensing that a child is ready before the child feels ready, so the audience feels quiet destiny wrapped in tenderness.
scenes.sceneShotName: Aida’s Sanctuary — morning shoreline crystal-bowl wide
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal bowl at the wave-lapped edge of the sanctuary, positioned between sea and forest path
scenes.colorTemperature: warm golden morning with soft rose-quartz tenderness
scenes.cameraHeight: seated-bear eye-level, gently looking across the bowl toward the forest path

scenes.name: The Pier Goodbye
scenes.location: Crystal Cove’s wooden pier where the dock meets open sparkling water, with the shore behind and a small sailboat tied alongside ready to push off toward the wider sea.
scenes.time: Late morning, continuing forward from the sanctuary vision.
scenes.weather: Clear and bright but with a light breeze beginning to worry the pier ropes.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden morning, bright blue water sparkle, soft maternal warmth with just enough open-sky brightness to make the goodbye ache.
scenes.look: An empty late-morning pier dominates the frame, its weathered wooden planks running from foreground shore toward open water at frame-right. A neat little sailboat is moored beside the dock, bow angled toward the sea, rope tied within easy reach. Inside the boat, the rolled map, satchel, berry pouch, and blanket sit visibly packed and orderly. A small Mum’s bag rests on the dock near the goodbye position. Pier posts, gently stirring ropes, and bright rippling water leave clear space for the boat to drift away, for a dolphin to surface beside it, and for the shore to recede behind. Nothing else intrudes; the frame holds the warm safety of home and the aching pull of departure.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the ache of a first goodbye, where Keen performs confidence while love gives him something real to carry.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove pier — late-morning goodbye, boat moored wide
scenes.definingFeature: the small packed sailboat tied to the sunlit pier, pointed toward the wide open water
scenes.colorTemperature: warm golden late-morning safety, lightly edged by cool aquamarine sea breeze
scenes.cameraHeight: child-eye dock level looking along the pier toward open water

scenes.name: At Sea
scenes.location: The vast open water beyond Crystal Cove, an endless mid-sea stretch with no land visible, built around a small sailboat on roughening waves beneath a fast-moving storm front.
scenes.time: Midday, after Keen has left the pier.
scenes.weather: Clear sea shifting rapidly into dark storm clouds, gusting wind, heavy rain, roughening waves, distant lightning.
scenes.lighting: Begins bright open blue, then gold drains into cold blue-grey storm light; visibility collapses into rain sheets and low contrast.
scenes.look: An empty small sailboat sits midground slightly left of frame, angled toward screen-right with mast, flapping sail, tiller, low rail, and secured map and supplies visible inside; the open sea dominates the frame in all directions, with bright aquamarine water in the near foreground turning steel-grey toward the horizon, choppy waves rising around the boat, rain curtains advancing from background right, dark clouds swallowing the sky, and distant lightning far behind the boat. No characters. Nothing on the water except the boat, waves, rain, and storm horizon.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about Keen discovering that pretending to know the way is not the same as being safe, so the audience feels the stomach-drop of being lost and the fragile relief of trusting a friend.
scenes.sceneShotName: Open sea — storm front swallowing tiny empty sailboat, waterline wide
scenes.definingFeature: the enormous storm front swallowing the horizon around one tiny sailboat
scenes.colorTemperature: cool storm-washed blue-grey fear with aquamarine undertone
scenes.cameraHeight: low waterline angle looking slightly up at the boat and storm

scenes.name: Rainforest Edge Lookout
scenes.location: The thinning edge of the rainforest above Crystal Cove, where wind-bent branches and wet leaves open into a narrow sightline toward the stormy sea and beach route off screen RIGHT.
scenes.time: Midday, continuous with the storm at sea.
scenes.weather: Wind strengthening, rain beginning through the trees, branches shivering and swaying hard.
scenes.lighting: Cool dim storm spill through canopy, wet leaves catching silver-grey highlights, distant sea barely visible in low contrast.
scenes.look: An empty, rain-threaded rainforest edge dominated by swaying foreground leaves and shivering branches, framing a broken gap through the trees toward the grey storm sea beyond; the open flight path runs LEFT-to-RIGHT toward the beach, with a tiny distant boat barely visible rising and falling in the far rain haze. Wet bark, trembling leaf clusters, wind-streaked rain, and the distant choppy horizon are the only elements; no characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about comedy snapping into care when someone is in danger, so the audience feels the laugh catch in their throat.
scenes.sceneShotName: Rainforest Edge Lookout — storm-lashed tree gap, bee's-eye wide
scenes.definingFeature: a wind-torn tree-gap lookout that reveals the tiny boat in the distant storm
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-grey storm light with drained midday green — the laugh catching into concern
scenes.cameraHeight: bee-height at the rainforest edge, slightly low looking through the branch gap toward the sea

scenes.name: Crystal Cove Beacon
scenes.location: The exposed Crystal Cove beach at the waterline, where wet sand, bent shore grasses and a rain-lashed view of the open sea create a clear gathering space facing the storm.
scenes.weather: Storm building hard over the cove, rain and wind pressing inland from the sea.
scenes.lighting: Cold storm-blue ambience over the beach, warm crystal glows pushing back against the grey when the group unites, beacon light cutting through rain.
scenes.look: An empty wide establishing plate of the stormy shoreline: wet sand dominates the foreground with a shell, scattered beach debris and a looping piece of driftwood positioned for a fast crash path; a clear open half-circle of shoreline sits midframe for the gathered group; wind-bent grasses edge the beach; beyond them, dark waves and rain fill the background with a strong sightline out to sea where a beacon can later cut through the storm. No characters, no boat visible, only the storm, shore, debris and open sea needed for the action.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the first circle of belonging reaching toward Keen before he knows their names, so the audience feels that help can be a light in the storm rather than a rescue from risk.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shoreline — storm-dark beacon beach wide
scenes.definingFeature: the rain-lashed waterline opening straight onto the storm-dark sea, framed by wind-bent grasses and crash-path driftwood
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-grey storm light with a faint muted aquamarine sea glow — belonging reaching through fear
scenes.cameraHeight: low shore-level, slightly below bear eye-line, looking out across the wet sand toward the sea

scenes.name: Storm Rescue
scenes.location: Crystal Cove at the storm-lashed shoreline, looking out across rough open water where the rescue moves between the glowing shore, the small sailboat, the wave troughs, and the submerged rocks just below the breaker line.
scenes.weather: Full storm, heavy rain, rough waves, massive swell, underwater current and foam.
scenes.lighting: Darkest point of the episode: storm-grey surface, cold underwater blue, aquamarine self-glow from Keen's vacant cuffs becoming the emotional light before the crystal exists.
scenes.look: An empty storm-rescue stage with wet Crystal Cove shore in the foreground for the watching line, violent grey waves dominating the midground, a small storm-tossed sailboat pitching beyond the breaker line, and a distant combined crystal beacon cutting through rain from shore toward the sea. The water near the foreground is clear enough in broken foam to reveal a submerged rock shelf and ragged drift net below the surface, ready for the underwater struggle. Heavy rain slants across the frame, massive swell rises screen-right to screen-left, and the shoreline crystal glow stays small but visible through the storm as the emotional way home. No characters.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about Keen dropping the performance of bravery and choosing love while afraid, so the audience feels the held breath of a child becoming brave in the only way that matters.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove shore and open sea — full storm rescue, low wide wave-line
scenes.definingFeature: the distant crystal beacon piercing the storm over towering waves and revealing the submerged rescue hazard beneath the breaker line
scenes.colorTemperature: cool blue-grey storm fear with faint aquamarine crystal hope
scenes.cameraHeight: low wave-height from the shore, looking slightly upward into the swell

scenes.name: Crystal Cove Welcome
scenes.location: Crystal Cove’s sheltered beach at the wet-sand edge, where active surf rolls in from frame-left/background and the cove opens into a broad receiving space framed by crystal-veined rock and glowing shoreline crystals.
scenes.time: Early afternoon, immediately after the rescue.
scenes.weather: Storm easing, rain slackening, clouds beginning to break though the sea remains active.
scenes.lighting: Cool storm light warming from within, wet sand reflecting soft gold and crystal colour, gentle sheltering brightness around the group.
scenes.look: Empty wide plate of the rescue landing place: shallow foamy water and rippled wet sand dominate the foreground, with a clear open landing area large enough for a welcoming circle at mid-frame; the restless sea sits behind it, still choppy but calming, while broken storm clouds part overhead and soft light spills onto the beach. Crystal clusters along the cove wall and shoreline glow gently, creating a sheltered, receiving feeling without crowding the action. No characters, no boat, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about a lost child being received without being made small, so the audience feels relief, welcome, and the first warmth of new family.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove beach — storm-eased shallows, welcoming wide
scenes.definingFeature: the crystal-veined cove wall curving around the wet-sand shallows like a natural shelter
scenes.colorTemperature: cool grey storm-light warming toward soft aquamarine-gold safety
scenes.cameraHeight: low child-height at the wet-sand edge, slightly looking toward the sheltered cove

scenes.name: Aquamarine Welcome Circle
scenes.location: A washed-clean circular gathering space in Crystal Cove, bordered by wet crystal-veined leaves, low glowing stones, and soft rainforest greenery, with a clear open center large enough for a witnessed welcome, applause, and a group hug.
scenes.time: Late afternoon, after the storm has passed.
scenes.weather: Fresh post-storm stillness, droplets on leaves, clouds clearing into sunlight.
scenes.lighting: Hopeful warm sun returning after rain, droplets sparkling, aquamarine light forming at the centre and settling into Keen's cuffs.
scenes.look: Empty wide establishing plate of the whole circular clearing: the open center dominates the frame, ringed by low crystals and broad rain-wet leaves that sparkle with droplets. The circle reads clearly as a place for bears to stand around a central space, with room for a central two-hander and a full group surge inward. Late-afternoon sunlight breaks through clearing clouds from upper left, catching water beads and carving a gentle halo around the empty center. Foreground has a few soft wet leaves and crystal edges for depth; midground is the clear circle floor; background is the enclosing post-storm rainforest and clearing sky. No characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about naming what was already true inside Keen, so the audience feels that courage is not awarded to him but recognised by a family who witnessed it.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove gathering circle — post-storm welcome circle, warm wide
scenes.definingFeature: the empty circular welcome space framed by rain-bright leaves and low crystal stones, waiting to recognise Keen at its center
scenes.colorTemperature: warm late-afternoon post-storm gold with fresh aquamarine crystal glints
scenes.cameraHeight: bear eye-level, slightly low, looking gently into the circle

scenes.name: Honey Friendship Walk
scenes.location: Crystal Cove’s peaceful shoreline path where wet golden sand meets the calm evening sea, with enough open beach for a loose group walking screen RIGHT along the waterline.
scenes.weather: Storm gone, air washed clean, settled breeze, calm sea.
scenes.lighting: Warm golden-hour glow, brighter and warmer than the morning, with a faint aquamarine memory in the reflections and rain still clinging to leaves.
scenes.look: Empty wide plate of the beach walk route: wet reflective sand dominates the foreground and midground as a clear horizontal path leading from frame LEFT toward screen RIGHT; gentle waves lap softly along the lower edge of the shoreline; the calm sea stretches beyond, washed clean after the storm; the cove curves quietly in the background with subtle crystal-veined rock catching evening light. The frame is open, warm, and breathable, staged specifically for a group to move together toward screen RIGHT with small hovering action beside the walking path; no characters, no extra props.
scenes.emotionalCore: This scene is really about the first ordinary joke after a life-changing day, so the audience feels that belonging is not a grand speech but someone already planning snacks with you.
scenes.sceneShotName: Crystal Cove beach — golden-hour shoreline walk, wide eye-level plate
scenes.definingFeature: the long reflective ribbon of wet golden sand beside the calm sea, leading the friendship walk screen RIGHT
scenes.colorTemperature: warm golden-hour amber safety, storm-washed and settled
scenes.cameraHeight: gentle eye-level at walking height, slightly open toward the shoreline

beats.storyBeat: The flower corridor is warm, safe, and busy; Zenny gathers pollen with neat precision while Fuzzby turns the same job into a full-speed performance, crashes into a leaf, and proudly buttons it as success.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel instant sunny playfulness: this world is delightful, Fuzzby is ridiculous, and the day still feels safe.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to perform competence and look like the most official pollen collector in the rainforest.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to be accepted as lovable even when his effort comes out messy, loud, and imperfect.
beats.crystalTruth: No personal crystals in frame; the ambient rainforest crystals stay steady-warm, telling the truth that this is a safe belonging-space even while Fuzzby performs perfection.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby buzzes like a tiny show-off, smacks into a leaf, and thinks he did brilliantly.
beats.adultRead: The joke is a child overperforming competence while needing permission to be messy and still belong.
beats.theGame: Pollen Run Rules: collect from each flower like it is a serious official mission; Fuzzby secretly thinks style points count more than accuracy.
beats.physicalFeeling: A warm whoosh through flowers that turns into a sudden soft belly-bonk.
beats.light: Warm golden morning god-rays isolate the tall pollen flowers and wrap both bees in safety; tiny ambient crystals glint softly in the grass without competing.
beats.atmosphere: Floating pollen motes thicken in the sunbeams, making the air feel playful and tickly; petals sway gently before Fuzzby disrupts them.
beats.motionTempo: Zenny is smooth and measured; Fuzzby is full-throttle, zig-zagging, over-accelerating, and constantly correcting after impacts; airborne wings flap rapidly and continuously.
beats.grade: Saturated warm amber-gold with friendly greens and honey pollen highlights, preserving the safe Spark feeling after the bonk.

cuts.framing: wide establishing, 24mm, slightly low, gentle organic push through towering flowers
cuts.action: Tall flowers sway in the warm breeze as Fuzzby and Zenny weave between blooms collecting pollen; Zenny works in smooth precise paths while Fuzzby zig-zags wildly through the air, humming loudly as he works.
cuts.delivery: fun sing-song rhythm; proud, breathless, self-conducted like a parade tune; wings beating rapidly throughout hover and flight

cuts.framing: medium tracking on Fuzzby, 35mm, handheld-breathing camera trying to keep up
cuts.action: Fuzzby dips low into a flower, scoops up pollen, overdoes the exit, spins sideways, and hits a leaf with a soft FWIP.
cuts.delivery: gag clock wind-up into exaggerated action; impact has soft mass, squash, overshoot, and quick pollen puff

cuts.framing: locked medium two-shot, Fuzzby frame-left and Zenny frame-right, eye level
cuts.action: Fuzzby bounces back into the air and straightens up proudly while Zenny remains neat and still enough to make his chaos look bigger.
cuts.delivery: tiny dignified cover-up after the bang; one proud chest-forward beat before the line; hold 10 frames after the line

beats.cameraArc: Begin with the living rainforest as playground, chase Fuzzby’s chaotic path, then stop dead on his proud cover-up so the laugh lands in stillness.
beats.pauseHold: Hold on Fuzzby’s proud recovery after “Nailed it.” for about 0.5 seconds while Zenny’s stillness quietly judges him.

performance.surface: Fuzzby is all pomp and official busy-bee confidence; Zenny is calm, efficient, and unreadably precise.
performance.underneath: Fuzzby is working hard to make mess look like mastery; Zenny already knows the mess is part of his charm.
performance.innerThought: Fuzzby: I am absolutely the professional here. Zenny: That leaf had no chance.

beats.crystalGlow: No personal crystal glow; ambient flower-bed crystals are steady warm, reflecting safety and playful belonging.
beats.startState: Opening frame: Fuzzby holds frame-left mid-hover near a tall open flower, poised as if about to launch into his work; Zenny holds frame-right beside another bloom, calmly set for precise pollen collecting. Both are airborne with wings already visibly beating rapidly. Tall flowers fill foreground and background in warm morning light.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush/fuzzy surfaces, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Deep within the rainforest flower corridor scene plate: towering soft-necked flowers, high grass, bright open pollen petals, layered leaves and branches, warm breeze implied by gentle petal lean, 2-3 subtle ambient crystals in the grass as soft bokeh. Fuzzby as per the reference image of Fuzzby, positioned frame-left; Zenny as per the reference image of Zenny, positioned frame-right, clearly smaller than Fuzzby. Opening frame only: both held airborne at the start of pollen collecting, wings in rapid visible flap. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens 24mm wide to make the bees feel tiny inside a magical flower maze; camera height slightly low at flower-stem level; composition uses tall vertical flower necks for scale and warm horizontal grass layers for safety; Fuzzby on left third, Zenny on right third; warm golden canopy key from upper-left, cool soft forest fill, shallow depth of field with pollen bokeh, warm rim light motivated by sun through petals.
beats.i2vPrompt: Anchor Image is TRUTH. COPY EXACTLY. Multi-shot sequence with clean cuts between shots. Shot 1 (wide establishing, 24mm, slightly low, gentle organic push through towering flowers): tall flowers sway gently in warm breeze; Fuzzby and Zenny weave between them collecting pollen from flower to flower; Zenny moves with smooth calm precision, Fuzzby flies full-throttle chaotic zig-zags, humming loudly; all airborne bee wings beat rapidly and continuously. FUZZBY: "BIZZY-BIZZY-BIZZY, BIZZY-BIZZY-BIZZY…" Shot 2 (cut to medium tracking on Fuzzby, 35mm, handheld-breathing): Fuzzby dips low into a flower, scoops up pollen, overdoes it, spins sideways into a leaf; FWIP impact with soft weight and pollen puff. Shot 3 (cut to locked medium two-shot, Fuzzby frame-left, Zenny frame-right): Fuzzby bounces back into the air, straightens proudly, chest forward in a tiny superhero stance; Zenny stays composed. FUZZBY: "Nailed it." Hold the proud cover-up for the button. Dialogue written in for lip-sync; voice swapped to canonical ElevenLabs in Post. Style+Audio: 3D CGI Pixar style; SFX only, no final music; warm breeze, pollen sparkle, rapid wing buzz, leaf FWIP, soft bounce, tiny proud silence before button; Fuzzby comic buzz motif only as timing texture. Duration: 12 seconds. Ratio: 16:9. --no text, watermarks, logos
beats.soundIntent: Warm rainforest bed with gentle breeze, soft pollen shimmer, rapid continuous bee-wing buzz, exaggerated FWIP leaf impact with cushioned mass, micro-silence before “Nailed it.”, then a tiny comic sting timed to Fuzzby’s proud cover-up; no final music.

continuity.opensFrom: Scene opens from black into warm golden morning inside the rainforest flower corridor.
continuity.carryToNext: Fuzzby’s inflated confidence and Zenny’s dry stillness carry forward; pollen has begun to dust the working flowers and the world remains warm and safe.

check.focalSubject: Fuzzby
check.emotionalRead: A safe sunny laugh with a soft physical bonk in the belly.
check.heartCheck: The child laughs at the buzzing crash and proud button; the parent recognises the tenderness of a little performer pretending every mistake was intentional.

beats.shot_style: 3D CGI feature-animation comedy, warm organic rainforest camera
beats.gag_carry: Fuzzby’s proud cover-up after obvious clumsiness continues into the next pollen gag.
beats.director_mode: Faithful adapter; BIG comedy staged with weight and affection.
beats.audience_feeling_target: Immediate delight and trust in the world before the storm edge appears.
beats.emotional_function: Tile 1 establish + Tile 3 hook + Tile 4 action + Tile 8 reaction-button.
beats.script_truth_lock: Uses action elements [0]-[4] and dialogue [1], [3] exactly once, in order.
beats.music_emotion: No final music; only rhythmic SFX timing and faint comic texture.
beats.performance_notes: Fuzzby’s flight is always fast, manic, hyper, and clumsy; Zenny’s stillness is the counter-joke. Airborne wings never stop flapping.
beats.physical_staging_intent: Make the leaf impact feel soft but weighty; Fuzzby never sinks through flowers or hangs with frozen wings.
beats.visibility_rule: Both bees visible from opening frame; Fuzzby frame-left, Zenny frame-right in shared shots.
beats.contact_rule: Fuzzby contacts only the leaf and flower surfaces described by the script; no added props attached to bodies.
beats.physics_rule: Impacts have squash, overshoot, settle, and recovery; static flowers and background crystals hold position except for wind sway.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: Button is Fuzzby’s proud upright pose after the obvious leaf crash.
beats.failed_correction_rule: If the render makes Fuzzby graceful or slow, reroll with full-throttle chaotic zig-zag and rapid wing-flap emphasis.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Pollen may dust Fuzzby and flowers as environmental material; do not add new permanent body accessories.
beats.prohibited_staging: No bears, no beach, no boat, no storm rain yet, no invented characters, no still airborne wings.

beats.storyBeat: Zenny glides beside Fuzzby and watches; Fuzzby escalates his official-worker act by plunging his whole face into a flower and emerging dusted in pollen, asking for validation that Zenny punctures with one dry line.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the tickly joy of a joke landing between two friends who know each other perfectly.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants Zenny to confirm that his messy overcommitment looks professional.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs affection that does not require him to be impressive; Zenny needs to let her fondness crack through the deadpan.
beats.crystalTruth: No personal crystals in frame; ambient crystals remain steady-warm, while pollen sparkle around Fuzzby makes the truth visible: he is funniest when he stops looking polished.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby gets a giant pollen moustache and asks if he looks official; Zenny calls him nuts.
beats.adultRead: A child’s need for approval is met by teasing that is affectionate, not cruel; Zenny’s almost-laugh shows connection beneath dryness.
beats.theGame: Official Bee Inspection: Fuzzby presents himself for an imaginary uniform check; Zenny is the deadpan inspector who grades the mess honestly.
beats.physicalFeeling: A tickly pollen puff right in the nose, followed by the squeeze of trying not to laugh.
beats.light: Warm amber light catches the pollen cloud around Fuzzby’s face like glitter, while Zenny’s face stays cleanly readable in softer frontal light for the deadpan punchline.
beats.atmosphere: Pollen hangs thicker after Fuzzby pulls out of the flower; the air sparkles around the joke and briefly stills for Zenny’s held reaction.
beats.motionTempo: Fuzzby rockets in and out of the flower with too much force; Zenny glides in calmly, then locks almost still for the line; wings beat rapidly whenever airborne.
beats.grade: Warm honey-gold and saturated flower colour, preserving playful safety and keeping the joke bright rather than mean.

beats.storyBeat: Aida sits beside the crystal bowl as the cove quiets; her Rose Quartz glow opens an inner vision of Keen and his Mum at the pier, then she returns, understands, says “It’s time,” and leaves for the forest.
beats.emotionalIntent: Quiet destiny wrapped in tenderness: the audience should feel the hush before a child’s first big step, and the elder’s gentle certainty that the moment has arrived.
beats.want: Aida performs stillness and listening rather than intervening too soon; she reaches for the exact moment when guidance is needed, not control.
beats.need: She needs to trust the vision and move with purpose, holding both Keen’s readiness and his Mum’s worry without rushing either of them.
beats.crystalTruth: Aida’s face remains calm and knowing, but her Rose Quartz pendant brightens with the truth underneath: love is being called into motion. Read: steady warm pink → soft brightening during the vision → steady-warm-but-changed as she returns.
beats.kidRead: A magic bowl hums, pink light shows Aida a secret picture, and now she knows where to go.
beats.adultRead: A parent’s letting-go moment is being witnessed by someone wise enough to honour the ache without stopping the child’s growth.
beats.theGame: The Listening Bowl: the rule is that the cove must become quiet enough for the heart-picture to appear; Aida wins by listening, not by forcing.
beats.physicalFeeling: A soft held breath, like the whole cove has paused to listen.
beats.light: Warm rose-gold morning isolates Aida in a gentle pool of shore-reflected light; the Rose Quartz glow softly rises across her face and becomes the emotional key, while the rest of the cove falls quieter and slightly cooler at the edges.
beats.atmosphere: Fine sea mist, warm water shimmer, and 2–3 subtle ambient crystals hold a breathing stillness; during the vision, rose-quartz haze gathers at the frame edges and then thins back into coastal air with the first hint of distant wind.
beats.motionTempo: Very slow and sustained: wand circling, eyelids closing, glow blooming, vision coalescing, then a small decisive rise. The movement lands on stillness before the line.
beats.grade: Rose-lavender tenderness over warm morning amber, with soft teal coastal fill preserved in shadows so the destiny feels gentle rather than grand.

cuts.framing: honest 50mm close-up, locked and still as the light becomes the movement
cuts.action: Aida closes her eyes. Her Rose Quartz pendant begins to glow, and soft pink light rises across her face.

cuts.framing: Aida POV vision filling the entire frame, 50mm dream-compressed view through rose-pink haze at the outer margins
cuts.action: AIDA’S INNER VISION — THROUGH AIDA’S EYES: a wooden pier, a small sailboat waiting on bright blue water, Keen standing beside his Mum; he checks the straps on his satchel, then checks them again, then one more time, while his Mum watches him with a proud smile that cannot quite hide her worry.

cuts.framing: cut back to Aida in medium close 50mm, locked for the reaction hold, then a gentle widening as she rises toward the forest path
cuts.action: BACK TO AIDA. The pink glow fades gently from her eyes. Aida places a paw over her heart with a soft knowing smile, then rises gracefully; the bowl’s note fades into the sound of distant wind as she turns and heads purposefully into the forest.
cuts.delivery: softly; almost breathed, certain but not dramatic, with the line landing after the paw reaches her heart
cuts.voiceTreatment: canonical Aida voice in post; spoken name pronunciation not needed in this line

performance.surface: Aida is serene, measured, and fully present; every gesture is small and deliberate.
performance.underneath: She feels the tender weight of knowing a child is about to step beyond home, and she chooses to meet that moment with care rather than alarm.
performance.innerThought: There you are. The world is opening, and someone must be waiting with warmth when you arrive.

beats.grade: Desaturated blue-grey storm grade with low contrast and sharp white rain streaks; the image preserves the cold aftershock.

cuts.framing: wide boat action shot, 24mm, sudden handheld-style reframe with ocean tilt
cuts.action: A gust of wind hits. The sail FLAPS hard. The boat lurches. Keen stumbles sideways and grabs the mast.
cuts.delivery: short involuntary yelp, no bravado left for one instant.

cuts.framing: medium storm-building shot, 35mm, pushing tighter as rain begins
cuts.action: The sky darkens. Clouds roll in faster than Keen expected. Squeaky disappears beneath the water. Another gust hits, stronger. Rain starts as a few drops, then many, then sheets. The sea roughens. The boat CREAKS. Keen tries to keep hold of the map while steering.

cuts.framing: insert/action close-up, 85mm, sharp snap-pan following the map for one beat
cuts.action: The wind SNATCHES the map from Keen’s paws. He lunges for it. Misses. The map whips through the air and vanishes into the storm.
cuts.delivery: panicked rush that cuts off into the miss; breath catches on the dash.

cuts.framing: locked 50mm close-up on Keen, eye-level, rain crossing foreground
cuts.action: Keen stands frozen. Rainwater sloshes around his feet. The horizon disappears behind sheets of grey.
cuts.delivery: under his breath; the first words small, then “I’m lost!!!” breaks through before he can stop it.

beats.cameraArc: The camera goes from unstable wide physical danger to a locked close-up where the real danger lands inside Keen.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 2 seconds after “I’m lost!!!” on Keen frozen in rain, with the horizon gone.

performance.surface: Keen fights to keep control: grab mast, hold map, steer, recover.
performance.underneath: The performance finally cracks; he is a child alone in a storm.
performance.innerThought: Mum isn’t here. The map is gone. I don’t know what to do.

beats.crystalGlow: Keen’s vacant wristbands remain dim and unlit, visible when his paws grip and fail to hold the map.
beats.startState: Keen is in the sailboat with the map in his paws and one paw close to the mast. The sail is held at a sharp angle just before the gust lands. Squeaky is surfaced near the boat. The horizon is darker than before, with clouds gathered behind the open water.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Opening frame for Scene 4 At Sea storm turn: as per the reference image of Keen in vacant-wristband state, and as per the reference image of Squeaky. Open sea scene plate with small sailboat exposed on choppy water, no shore, no beach, no pier. Keen is in the boat holding the map, body braced near the mast at the instant before the gust. Squeaky is surfaced near the boat. Clouds have gathered; the horizon is darkening. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens 24mm wide for physical lurch and vulnerability; camera movement implied as sudden handheld-style ocean reframe; camera height low inside the boat looking slightly up at the sail and storm sky; composition uses diagonal mast and sail lines to break the earlier calm horizon; key light cold blue-silver from storm clouds, motivated by overcast sky; fill light dim teal from rough water; depth of field deeper so rain, sail, and horizon all read; light direction side-top storm spill, making Keen feel exposed. Add 2–3 subtle ambient crystal glints as faint aquamarine flecks in storm spray, nearly swallowed by grey.
beats.i2vPrompt: Anchor Image is TRUTH. COPY EXACTLY. Multi-shot sequence with clean cuts between shots. Shot 1 (wide boat action, 24mm, sudden handheld-style reframe): a gust of wind hits. The sail FLAPS hard. The boat lurches. Keen stumbles sideways and grabs the mast. KEEN: "Oh!" Shot 2 (cut to medium storm-building shot, 35mm, push tighter): the sky darkens. Keen tries to keep hold of the map while steering. Shot 3 (cut to insert/action close-up, 85mm, sharp snap-pan): the wind SNATCHES the map from Keen’s paws. He lunges for it. Misses. The map whips through the air and vanishes into the storm. KEEN: "No no no no—!" Shot 4 (cut to locked 50mm close-up, eye-level, rain foreground): Keen stands frozen. Rainwater sloshes around his feet. The horizon disappears behind sheets of grey. KEEN: "Oh no… I’m lost!!!" Dialogue written in for lip-sync; voice forward, final voice swapped in Post. Small clean motion per cut except the storm lurch; boat and mast keep consistent geometry; map exits frame and is gone; Squeaky is no longer visible after disappearing under the water. Duration 15 seconds. Ratio 16:9. --no text, watermarks, logos
beats.soundIntent: Hard sail FLAP, hull CREAK, rain escalating from sparse drops to sheets, wind ripping the map away, then sound narrows under “Oh no…” so the lost admission lands. Music is Post only: Keen’s B note becomes thin and isolated.

continuity.opensFrom: Keen ends the prior beat holding the upside-down map with uncertainty exposed; this opens with that uncertainty becoming weather.
continuity.carryToNext: Map is lost permanently; Squeaky is no longer visible; storm is full; Keen is frozen, wet, gripping for control; vacant wristbands remain unlit.

check.focalSubject: Keen losing the map and admitting he is lost.
check.emotionalRead: The adventure has tipped into real danger; confidence is no longer enough.
check.heartCheck: The child feels the scary surprise of the storm stealing the map; the parent feels the first real cost of letting a child face the world.

beats.writerNote: The line “I’m lost!!!” states the condition directly, but this is a locked final script; staging must make the loss read through map, horizon, rain and Keen’s frozen body so the line does not carry the feeling alone.
beats.shot_style: Storm escalation: physical lurch into locked emotional close-up.
beats.director_mode: TRUE heart beat: action is frightening but legible and not sensational.
beats.audience_feeling_target: Stomach-drop fear and the loneliness of the vanished horizon.
beats.emotional_function: Tile 7 climax of the scene’s collapse plus Tile 8 reaction hold on lost.
beats.script_truth_lock: Gust, sail flap, lurch, storm, Squeaky disappearance, map snatch, failed lunge, horizon vanishing and exact lost line all occur in order.
beats.music_emotion: Isolated unresolved B note, no final music from Seedance.
beats.performance_notes: Keen’s body should go from reactive scramble to total stillness; the stillness is the emotional impact.
beats.physical_staging_intent: One clean physical chain: lurch, grab, map snatch, miss, freeze.
beats.visibility_rule: Squeaky is visible in the opening frame and disappears beneath the water during Cut 2; do not show him again in this beat.
beats.contact_rule: Keen does not touch Squeaky; Keen loses contact with the map after the snatch.
beats.physics_rule: Wind direction must visibly pull sail, rain and map consistently; map vanishes into storm and does not return.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: The horizon disappearing behind grey sheets is the visual proof that Keen is lost.
beats.failed_correction_rule: Do not rescue the moment with comedy or a quick recovery; hold the fear.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Keen’s vacant wristbands stay on his wrists, dark and physically vacant; no glow before Scene 7.
beats.prohibited_staging: No crystal beacon, no beach, no other bears, no Squeaky resurfacing in this beat, no map remaining aboard.

beats.storyBeat: Keen grips the boat in the storm, breathes through fear, hears Squeaky return, and chooses to trust the dolphin’s direction, wrestling the boat around to follow.
beats.emotionalIntent: Feel a held breath unclench into fragile trust — not safety yet, but a way forward.
beats.want: Keen wants to steady himself by repeating the confidence he has used all day.
beats.need: Keen needs to let someone else help and make a brave choice while still frightened.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen’s vacant wristbands stay dim and unlit: the face says “I’ve got this,” but the absence of glow reveals he only begins to find steadiness when he listens and trusts.
beats.kidRead: Squeaky pops back through the storm and shows Keen which way to go.
beats.adultRead: A child’s first self-management is not instant calm; it is one breath, one trusted signal, one next choice.
beats.theGame: Follow-the-Clicks: the new rule replaces the lost map — when Squeaky clicks, Keen practices trusting a living friend over pretending he knows alone.
beats.physicalFeeling: A tight held breath in the ribs that loosens when Squeaky’s chatter returns.
beats.light: Cold blue-silver storm light remains dominant, but a faint aquamarine reflection catches the water around Squeaky when he reappears, isolating the possibility of trust without making it safe.
beats.atmosphere: Rain sheets continue; lightning flickers far away; storm spray briefly opens around Squeaky’s leap like a small moving doorway.
beats.motionTempo: Still grip, quickened breath, one deliberate inhale, alert eye-open, then committed but effortful turning of the boat.
beats.grade: Blue-grey storm grade preserved, with a faint aquamarine lift only around the return path so relief stays fragile.

cuts.framing: dedicated fear close-up, 85mm, locked on Keen’s paws and face edge at boat rail
cuts.action: Lightning flickers far away. Keen grips the edge of the boat tighter. His breathing quickens. He closes his eyes for just a second. Breathes. Grounds himself.
cuts.dialogue: KEEN: I’ve got this… I’ve got this…
cuts.delivery: quiet, steadying; the first phrase is for survival, the second is smaller and more honest.

cuts.framing: medium close-up on Keen, 50mm, locked then tiny eye-line shift toward sound
cuts.action: A familiar CLICKING CHATTER sounds nearby. Keen opens his eyes.

cuts.framing: wide storm two-shot, 35mm, low waterline track with rain slicing foreground
cuts.action: Squeaky leaps through the storm water beside the boat. He dips and reappears, signaling insistently in one direction.
cuts.delivery: uncertain question, breath still shaky; do not make it heroic yet.

cuts.framing: exit wide, 24mm, boat-level, strong rightward movement following Squeaky’s lead
cuts.action: Squeaky chirps. Keen looks at the rough water. Then at Squeaky. Then nods. Squeaky surges ahead. Keen wrestles the little boat around to follow.
cuts.delivery: decision through fear; a small lift of courage, not a triumphant shout.

beats.cameraArc: Locked fear close-up holds Keen with himself, then opens outward as Squeaky’s return gives him a path to follow.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 1.5 seconds between Keen looking at the rough water and looking back to Squeaky before the nod; this is the fear-before-choice beat.

performance.surface: Keen repeats his brave phrase and chooses action.
performance.underneath: He is still scared, and the choice works only because he lets Squeaky matter.
performance.innerThought: I don’t know the way. But he might. I can follow.

beats.crystalGlow: Keen’s vacant wristbands remain dim and unlit; no crystal glow yet. The emotional light comes from the faint aquamarine storm reflection around Squeaky’s return.
beats.startState: Keen is alone in the visible opening frame, inside the storm-tossed sailboat. He is at the boat rail with both paws gripping the edge, eyes closed, shoulders held tight. Rainwater sits around his feet. Squeaky is not visible in the opening frame.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Opening frame for Scene 4 At Sea grounding beat: as per the reference image of Keen in vacant-wristband state. Squeaky is NOT visible in the opening frame. Open sea storm scene plate: small sailboat in heavy rain, rough water, grey horizon erased by sheets of rain, no shore, no beach, no pier, no other characters. Keen is inside the boat at the rail, paws gripping, eyes closed in a held fear-before-breath pose. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens 85mm close for held fear and intimacy; camera movement locked, because the courage must be seen before the action; camera height eye-level with Keen, tight enough for paws and breath to read; composition places Keen slightly low in frame with rain-streaked negative space above him; key light cold blue-silver from storm clouds, motivated by lightning-dim overcast; fill light dark teal bounce from wet boat and sea; depth of field shallow with rain foreground streaks soft; light direction side/top storm spill, making him exposed but readable. Add 2–3 subtle ambient aquamarine crystal glints as tiny blurred flecks in rain and sea spray, very faint.

Keen grips the edge of the boat tighter. His breathing quickens. He closes his eyes for just a second. Breathes. Grounds himself. Duration 15 seconds. Ratio 16:9. --no text, watermarks, logos
beats.soundIntent: Distant lightning flicker, rain on wood, Keen’s breath close and audible, then Squeaky’s familiar clicking cuts through the storm. Music is Post only: Keen’s B note steadies slightly when he nods, but does not resolve.

continuity.opensFrom: Keen ended the prior beat frozen and lost with the map gone, Squeaky absent, horizon erased.
continuity.carryToNext: Keen is following Squeaky through the storm, map lost, boat still intact for now, vacant wristbands unlit, trust beginning to replace performed certainty.

check.focalSubject: Keen’s fear-before-choice and the return of Squeaky as a trusted guide.
check.emotionalRead: He is not safe yet, but he is no longer pretending to be alone in control.
check.heartCheck: The child feels relief when Squeaky comes back; the parent feels the deeper truth that courage can begin as accepting help.

beats.shot_style: Fear close-up into fragile-trust exit wide; scene beauty moment in Squeaky’s return through rain.
beats.director_mode: TRUE heart beat with mandatory Keen fear shot before the brave choice to follow.
beats.audience_feeling_target: A held breath unclenching into trust.
beats.emotional_function: Tile 8 reaction hold from being lost plus Tile 9 exit as Keen follows Squeaky into the storm path.
beats.script_truth_lock: Lightning, grip, breath, steadying line, clicking chatter, Squeaky’s leap/signaling, question, chirp, look-look-nod, final line and boat turn all occur in order.
beats.music_emotion: Steadier but unresolved Keen B note; no final music from Seedance.
beats.performance_notes: The nod must be small and costly; Keen is not suddenly fearless, he is choosing through fear.
beats.physical_staging_intent: The central action is the breath, then the nod, then the effortful boat turn — no extra heroics.
beats.visibility_rule: Squeaky is absent from the opening frame and enters only after the clicking chatter in Shot 3.
beats.contact_rule: No physical contact; Keen follows Squeaky’s signal from the boat.
beats.physics_rule: Boat turn requires effort against wind and rough water; Squeaky’s surge creates a readable direction path.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: Beauty moment: Squeaky’s shape cuts through the rain with a faint aquamarine water reflection, a tiny living guide in the grey.
beats.failed_correction_rule: Do not make the return feel like an easy rescue; the storm remains dangerous and the relief fragile.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Keen’s vacant wristbands remain dark; they do not glow until the underwater rescue in Scene 7.
beats.prohibited_staging: No map recovery, no crystal beacon, no other bears, no shore visibility, no triumphant power-up.

beats.storyBeat: Wind and rain reach the rainforest edge as Fuzzby and Zenny hurry back through the trees; Fuzzby chatters ahead until he freezes mid-air, points through the rain, and sees the tiny boat rising and falling in the distant storm.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience begins laughing at Fuzzby’s unstoppable patter, then feels the laugh catch as the danger finally enters the frame.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to keep performing unshakable comic confidence, talking fast enough to outrun the storm feeling; Zenny wants to keep the route home orderly and contained.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to stop performing and let genuine concern interrupt him; Zenny needs to trust that his sudden stillness means something real.
beats.crystalTruth: No character crystal is present in this bee-only beat; the need is carried by the environment instead: tiny ambient crystals in the wet branches dim from warm pollen-gold to cool blue-silver as Fuzzby’s face stops pretending before his words catch up.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby is talking too much, then freezes so suddenly that Zenny almost bumps into him.
beats.adultRead: The instant a childlike clown goes silent, everyone understands the stakes have changed; care arrives before competence.
beats.theGame: Storm-Spotters: the playful rule is that whoever sees danger first must stop the game of buzzing and point, even if they were mid-brag.
beats.physicalFeeling: A small breath-stopping catch in the chest as motion suddenly disappears.
beats.light: Cool storm-blue light slips through the canopy and isolates Fuzzby’s frozen silhouette against rain-softened sea beyond; the last warm green-gold leaf highlights drain toward silver.
beats.atmosphere: Wind combs the leaves harder, rain begins as fine diagonal streaks, and wet pollen motes vanish into storm mist so the world feels like it has just taken a breath in.
beats.motionTempo: Fast, jittery bee flight lands on an abrupt dead stop, then stretches into a slow pointing gesture and a held look.
beats.grade: Desaturated grey-green rainforest with blue-silver storm spill, preserving a trace of earlier warmth only at the frame edges.

cuts.framing: wide bee-eye tracking two-shot, 24mm, organic handheld drift moving LEFT-to-RIGHT through the wind-lashed rainforest edge
cuts.action: The wind picks up around the trees. Leaves shiver. Branches sway harder now. Fuzzby and Zenny move quickly through the air, heading back through the forest. Fuzzby flies slightly ahead, wings beating rapidly and continuously, still talking as rain begins to thread between the leaves; Zenny follows frame-right, precise and controlled, wings also beating rapidly.
cuts.dialogue: FUZZBY: I’m just saying, if this turns into a full storm situation, I am extremely—
cuts.delivery: fast, pompous, breathless patter cut off mid-word by the visual discovery; comic confidence thinning under the wind noise

cuts.framing: cut to tight medium on Fuzzby, 50mm, locked-off sudden stillness with Zenny entering the edge of frame-right
cuts.action: He stops dead in mid-air. Completely still except for rapidly beating wings. Zenny nearly bumps into him and arrests herself just short, her tiny correction restrained and dry.
cuts.delivery: flat but genuinely alert, one small upward lift at the end; the deadpan now has a hairline crack of concern

cuts.framing: cut to over-Fuzzby-and-Zenny sightline through branches, 85mm compressed telephoto, slow rack focus from wet foreground leaves to distant storm sea
cuts.action: No response. Fuzzby slowly raises one tiny paw and points. Zenny follows his gaze. Out beyond the trees, through shifting wind and rain, a tiny boat rises and falls in the distance, barely visible in the storm. Beat. Fuzzby’s eyes go wide.
cuts.dialogue: FUZZBY: …that is NOT a good place to be.
cuts.delivery: small, hushed, the bravado gone; the pause before the line is the laugh catching in his throat

beats.cameraArc: The camera begins trying to keep up with Fuzzby’s manic speed, then becomes progressively still and longer-lensed as the danger pulls the comedy into care.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 1.5 seconds after Fuzzby points and before his line, long enough for the tiny boat to read through the rain and for the audience’s body to shift from giggle to concern.

performance.surface: Fuzzby is still the loud, self-important little professional; Zenny is composed and mildly unimpressed.
performance.underneath: Fuzzby’s real kindness arrives as silence before language; Zenny’s deadpan softens because she recognises the same danger he does.
performance.innerThought: Fuzzby: I was joking about storms, but that boat is real. Zenny: If Fuzzby has gone quiet, this is serious.

beats.crystalGlow: No bear crystal glow; ambient branch crystals and rain beads cool and dim subtly, contradicting Fuzzby’s attempted comic confidence by showing the need for care.
beats.startState: Fuzzby frame-left mid-hover slightly ahead of Zenny, facing RIGHT along the thinning rainforest edge, wings poised for rapid flapping; Zenny frame-right just behind him, also facing RIGHT, held in a neat controlled hover. Wind-bent leaves and branches surround them, with a broken sightline toward the distant storm sea beyond.
beats.keyframePrompt: Polished 3D CGI animation — modern feature-film CGI (Pixar/DreamWorks quality): fully 3D-modelled characters and environments, physically-based rendering, soft global illumination and volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering on plush fur, large expressive eyes with warm catch-lights, realistic materials, cinematic depth of field. NOT 2D, NOT hand-drawn, NOT flat. Rainforest Edge Lookout scene plate: wind-lashed edge of the rainforest where trees thin toward the coast, wet leaves, swaying branches, partial sightline to stormy ocean beyond; no flower-pollen clearing, no beach gathering, no boat in foreground. Add 2-3 subtle ambient crystals tucked into wet roots and branch hollows, soft blue-silver bokeh, not competing with the action. Characters as per the reference image of Fuzzby and as per the reference image of Zenny; Fuzzby is frame-left, Zenny frame-right, both visible in the opening frame, both in mid-hover with wings ready to beat rapidly. CINEMATOGRAPHY: Lens: 24mm — wide enough to feel the bee-scale corridor and wind pressure. Camera movement: organic tracking feel implied from left-to-right — the forest is alive and unstable. Camera height: bee-eye level, slightly below the branch line, looking through leaves toward the coast. Composition: Fuzzby on left third slightly ahead, Zenny on right third just behind, vertical tree trunks creating pressure, open storm sightline in background. Key light: cool blue-silver storm spill motivated by clouded sky through canopy. Fill light: soft wet green bounce from leaves, very subdued. Depth of field: moderate-shallow, bees sharp, distant storm softened. Light direction: side-rim from storm opening, making the edge of the world feel suddenly exposed. Opening frame only, before Fuzzby stops dead; no aftermath.

Shot 1 (wide bee-eye tracking two-shot, 24mm, organic handheld drift moving LEFT-to-RIGHT through the wind-lashed rainforest edge): wind picks up around the trees; leaves shiver; branches sway harder; Fuzzby and Zenny move quickly through the air heading back through the forest, Fuzzby slightly ahead, wings beating rapidly and continuously, Zenny frame-right behind him with rapidly beating wings. FUZZBY: "I’m just saying, if this turns into a full storm situation, I am extremely—" Shot 2 (cut to tight medium on Fuzzby, 50mm, locked-off sudden stillness): Fuzzby stops dead in mid-air, completely still except for rapid wingbeats; Zenny nearly bumps into him and stops just short. Shot 3 (cut to over-Fuzzby-and-Zenny sightline through wet branches, 85mm compressed telephoto, slow rack focus): no response; Fuzzby slowly raises one tiny paw and points; Zenny follows his gaze; through shifting wind and rain a tiny boat rises and falls in the distant storm, barely visible; hold the beat; Fuzzby’s eyes go wide. beats.soundIntent: Begin with fast Fuzzby wing-buzz and wind through leaves under his patter; cut the bed down sharply on his sudden stop, leaving rain ticks, rapid wing flutters, and a distant thunder bed. Let the distant boat reveal land on a low, unresolved storm tone; no joke sting, because the comedy has turned true.

continuity.opensFrom: Scene 4’s storm has moved inland; we open on the same midday storm pressure now reaching the rainforest edge as Fuzzby and Zenny head back from the earlier pollen run.
continuity.carryToNext: Fuzzby remains frozen frame-left with paw raised and eyes wide; Zenny frame-right has followed his gaze and her deadpan is beginning to soften toward action.

check.focalSubject: Fuzzby’s sudden stillness and pointing paw, with Zenny’s near-bump as the comic-to-care hinge.
check.emotionalRead: The audience should feel the physical drop from playful motion to alarm: something is wrong out there.
check.heartCheck: The child laughs at Fuzzby freezing and Zenny almost bumping him; the parent feels the instant when a silly character becomes a witness to real danger.

beats.shot_style: Bee-scale storm lookout with organic camera shifting into locked emotional stillness.
beats.gag_carry: Fuzzby’s interrupted brag lands not as a punchline but as a care trigger; carry his wide-eyed alarm into the emergency blast.
beats.director_mode: Docter TRUE beat: hold the laugh and the ache in the same breath; do not overplay the joke after the boat is visible.
beats.audience_feeling_target: A laugh caught in the throat.
beats.emotional_function: Establish + hook + reaction-hold: world pressure, sudden discovery, held recognition of danger.
beats.script_truth_lock: Use the distant boat only as barely visible through rain; no rescue action, no beach, no other characters.
beats.music_emotion: Sparse unresolved storm tone under natural SFX; Fuzzby’s comic rhythm drains away.
beats.performance_notes: Fuzzby’s wings keep rapidly beating while his body freezes; Zenny’s near-bump is tiny and precise, not broad slapstick.
beats.physical_staging_intent: Contrast manic forward motion with a dead stop so danger is felt through absence of motion.
beats.visibility_rule: The boat is distant and barely visible through trees, wind, and rain; it must not become a clear foreground object.
beats.contact_rule: Zenny nearly bumps into Fuzzby but does not collide with him.
beats.physics_rule: Airborne bees always maintain rapid continuous wingbeats; wind pushes leaves and rain, not the characters off-axis beyond readable staging.
beats.visual_payoff_rule: The beauty moment is the rain-softened telephoto reveal of the tiny boat rising and falling beyond the trees.
beats.failed_correction_rule: Do not turn Fuzzby’s freeze into a full crash or pratfall in this beat; the stillness is the point.
beats.continuity_physical_rule: Fuzzby remains frame-left, Zenny frame-right; both face RIGHT toward the beach/coast direction at scene open.
beats.prohibited_staging: No Crystal Bears, no beach gathering, no close-up boat rescue, no added props on bees, no stationary wings while airborne.

beats.storyBeat: Zenny names the danger, Fuzzby converts fear into action, screams for help, blasts toward the beach, immediately bonks into a branch, and keeps going anyway while Zenny follows faster than before.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience gets the release-laugh of Fuzzby’s emergency overdrive, but the laugh is powered by love: he is ridiculous because he cares so much.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to become an instant heroic alarm system and outrun his own panic; Zenny wants to focus the chaos into one clear next move.
beats.need: Fuzzby needs to let his messy urgency be useful instead of polished; Zenny needs to allow speed and concern to crack her perfect composure.
beats.crystalTruth: No character crystal is present; the wet ambient crystals flicker cooler-brighter as the need turns outward: not looking competent, but getting help now.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby yells emergency, zooms off like a rocket, bonks a branch, and keeps flying.
beats.adultRead: The same flaw that makes Fuzzby funny — too much, too fast — becomes exactly the trait that sends help toward a child in trouble.
beats.theGame: Emergency Messengers: the rule is that panic only counts if it moves toward help, even when it bonks on the way.
beats.physicalFeeling: A jolting comic BONK in the ribs, followed by relief that he keeps going.
beats.light: A cold blue-silver storm slash catches the branch just before impact, making the bonk read clearly; tiny ambient crystals pulse briefly on the word EMERGENCY then settle behind the motion.
beats.atmosphere: Rain thickens, leaves whip harder, and the path toward the beach opens like a fast, wet tunnel through the trees.
beats.motionTempo: Softening stillness snaps into explosive acceleration, impact, recoil, shake-off, and renewed forward drive.
beats.grade: Cool storm grey-green with bright wet highlights; preserve the urgency by keeping contrast higher on Fuzzby and the branch impact point.

cuts.framing: locked medium two-shot, 50mm, Fuzzby frame-left and Zenny frame-right against the rainy sea sightline
cuts.action: Zenny’s expression softens immediately as she understands what they are seeing; Fuzzby remains wide-eyed beside her, wings beating rapidly in place.
cuts.delivery: quiet, simple, deadpan stripped away into care; no melodrama, just truth landing

cuts.framing: cut to punchy close-medium on Fuzzby, 35mm, quick push-in timed to his snap into action
cuts.action: Fuzzby snaps into action, chest forward in proud emergency mode for one tiny wind-up before panic rockets through him; wings blur faster.
cuts.delivery: full-volume alarm, staccato and urgent, each BEARS punched like a siren; lovable panic, not fearmongering

cuts.framing: cut to wide full-body flight lane, 24mm, whip-pan follows Fuzzby’s blast toward the beach
cuts.action: He blasts off toward the beach at full speed. Immediately flies into a branch. BONK. He recoils with real squash-and-settle weight, shakes it off, and keeps going anyway; Zenny follows behind, calm and focused, this time faster, wings beating rapidly and continuously.
cuts.delivery: Gag clock: explosive action, massy bonk, eight-frame stunned take, tiny dignity recovery, then onward urgency

beats.cameraArc: Start locked and honest for Zenny’s care, punch into Fuzzby’s emergency siren, then let the camera barely keep up with his full-throttle chaotic blast and weighted branch bonk.
beats.pauseHold: Hold Fuzzby’s post-bonk stunned take for about 10 frames before he shakes it off and keeps going; the scene exits on motion, not sentiment.

performance.surface: Zenny is calm, focused, and briefly warmer; Fuzzby becomes a tiny emergency siren with too much speed and not enough steering.
performance.underneath: Both bees care immediately. Fuzzby’s chaos becomes courage-adjacent usefulness; Zenny’s stillness becomes purposeful speed.
performance.innerThought: Zenny: Say the truth, then move. Fuzzby: Get the bears now — dignity can catch up later.

beats.crystalGlow: No bear crystal glow; ambient rainforest crystals flicker in cool pulses as urgency becomes connection, then trail off behind the bees toward the beach.
beats.startState: Fuzzby frame-left held mid-hover with paw still lowered after pointing, eyes wide toward the distant storm sea; Zenny frame-right in a controlled hover, gaze following the same direction, expression just beginning to soften. Wet branches sway around them, with the open route toward the beach off to the RIGHT.

Opening frame only, before Fuzzby snaps into action and before the branch bonk.

beats.storyBeat: The Crystal Bears face the storm-dark sea as Fuzzby hurtles in too fast, fails to stop, pinballs through the beach debris, lands in a soggy heap, and saves his dignity with a tiny lifted paw.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience feels the jolt of alarm softened by a laugh: help is coming, but it arrives messy and lovable.
beats.want: Fuzzby wants to look like the heroic emergency messenger who meant every move.
beats.need: He needs to let his frantic care matter more than his dignity.
beats.crystalTruth: The nearby crystals and the bears’ steady glows hold concern rather than panic: Fuzzby’s face performs confidence, but the world’s light shows the need underneath — someone out there matters.
beats.kidRead: Fuzzby crashes spectacularly and still pretends it was on purpose.
beats.adultRead: Even messy, imperfect help can be real help when it comes from care.
beats.physicalFeeling: A sudden stomach-lurch as Fuzzby blasts into the frame faster than the eye can settle.
beats.light: Cold storm-blue shore light isolates the incoming chaos, with wet shells and driftwood catching sharp silver highlights at each impact; the gathered bears remain rimmed by faint warm crystal glows so the comedy stays safe.
beats.atmosphere: Rain needles sideways through the cove grasses; sea mist and spray thicken just enough to make Fuzzby’s arrival feel like he has been blown in by the storm itself.
beats.motionTempo: Wind-up stillness at the shore, then full-throttle zig-zag impact chaos, then an exaggerated dead-stop hold on the tiny paw.
beats.grade: Cool blue-grey storm grade with small warm crystal accents preserved around the bears, keeping fear and warmth in the same frame.

cuts.action: Beat. One tiny paw lifts from the heap while nobody moves to validate him.
cuts.dialogue: FUZZBY: I meant to do that.
cuts.delivery: tiny, proud, breath-squeezed dignity; the line lands after an 8–12 frame silence, as if restoring command of the room

beats.storyBeat: Keen grips the net with both paws as the wristbands glow brighter; he strains against the tangle under a massive wave, tears the net free, is thrown through dark foam, then spots Squeaky and calls him to stay with him.

beats.want: Keen wants the final pull to prove he can save Squeaky before the wave takes them.

beats.kidRead: Keen pulls super hard, the net snaps, a huge wave crashes, and he yells for Squeaky.
beats.adultRead: The parent sees that bravery does not end at the heroic act; after the snap comes disorientation, cost, and the need to stay connected.
beats.theGame: Wave-Countdown: Keen must free Squeaky before the giant wave takes its turn, and the winning move is not glory but keeping the friend in sight after the crash.
beats.physicalFeeling: A full-body clench, then the floor disappearing in white water.
beats.light: The aquamarine wristband glow intensifies around Keen’s paws against near-dark blue; when the wave crashes, the light fragments into foam and bubbles before re-gathering on his face as he sees Squeaky.
beats.atmosphere: The water thickens with bubbles, torn net fibers, and the shadow of the massive wave overhead; foam explodes into the frame after the snap.
beats.motionTempo: Slow strain, hard pull, instantaneous SNAP, violent tumble, then one sharp focus onto Squeaky.
beats.grade: Darkest blue-grey with high-contrast white foam and pulsing aquamarine fragments.

beats.storyBeat: Squeaky brings Keen safely into the shallows; Keen hops onto the sand, laughs through exhaustion, almost collapses, and Howey steadies him as the bears form a warm receiving circle around him.

beats.want: Keen wants to keep the rescue feeling like a triumphant adventure and prove he is fine.
beats.need: Keen needs to let his tired body be supported and discover that being helped does not make him smaller.

beats.kidRead: Keen got an amazing dolphin ride and his legs have turned wobbly.
beats.adultRead: A child who has just done something huge is allowed to be shaky afterward; the welcome preserves his dignity by steadying him without fuss.
beats.theGame: The Wobble Landing: the rule is that if your legs forget the ground, the circle catches you before anyone makes it a big deal.
beats.physicalFeeling: the knees-give-way relief of finally reaching shore
beats.light: Storm-blue beach light warms around Keen as broken cloud gold catches the wet sand; Howey’s step-in is isolated by a soft white crystal fill while the sea remains cooler behind them.
beats.atmosphere: Slackening rain, sea mist, and tiny droplets hang in the air; the surf still breathes heavily, but the beach particles glow warmer as the circle closes.
beats.motionTempo: A fast watery arrival drops into stumbling, wobbling, steadying, then a small held breath before Keen introduces himself.
beats.grade: Cool storm residue preserved in the shadows with soft gold and aquamarine warmth blooming only around the gathered welcome.

beats.adultRead: After a frightening event, even praise can be too much; real care sometimes means creating space, not filling it.

beats.grade: Storm-blue softened by beach gold, with saturation lifted on crystal glows only during the laugh so the comedy feels safe.

beats.continuity_physical_rule: Keen’s wristbands remain vacant with no aquamarine gems; the award happens only in Scene 9.
beats.prohibited_staging: No group hug, no crystal ceremony, no Squeaky departure yet in this beat.

beats.storyBeat: Keen turns back to the sea as Squeaky leaps once more; Aida quietly gestures a message home, Squeaky understands and leaves, and Keen receives the news that his Mum will know he is okay.
beats.emotionalIntent: Relief becomes bittersweet: Keen has found shelter, but love still stretches back across the sea to Mum.
beats.want: Keen wants to understand what Aida did and keep hold of the friend who brought him safely in.
beats.need: Keen needs reassurance that belonging here does not sever the bond with home.
beats.crystalTruth: Keen watches Squeaky go with a brave quiet face, but his vacant wristbands hold a soft steady ache; Aida’s rose quartz remains steady-warm, revealing the need to connect him back to Mum rather than simply celebrate his arrival.
beats.kidRead: Squeaky understands Aida’s gesture and swims off to tell Keen’s Mum he is okay.
beats.adultRead: The most loving welcome remembers the parent waiting at home; independence is not abandonment when connection travels both ways.
beats.theGame: Message-in-the-Waves: the rule is that a goodbye can carry a hello home, and the sea becomes a path for care instead of just distance.
beats.physicalFeeling: the soft throat-tight ache when someone says home has been told
beats.light: Warm rose-gold from Aida’s side gently reaches toward the cooler sea; Squeaky’s leap catches a brief silver-aquamarine highlight before he dives back into the waves.
beats.atmosphere: Rain slackens to near-stillness; a few droplets fall from leaves while the surf breathes quietly, making the pause after Aida’s line feel tender.
beats.motionTempo: Turn, leap, small gesture, understood dip, dive away, question, answer, grateful stillness, nod.
beats.grade: Bittersweet aquamarine and rose-gold: keep the sea cool and wide, while the beach around Keen stays warm enough to hold him.

cuts.framing: medium-wide over Keen toward the shallows, 50mm, slow pan with his turn back to the sea
cuts.action: He turns back toward the sea. Squeaky leaps once more just beyond the shallows. Aida steps forward beside Keen.

cuts.framing: clean three-point composition, 85mm compressed from beach to water, small locked gesture shot
cuts.action: Aida lifts a paw and makes a small, gentle gesture to the dolphin. Squeaky watches her. Seems to understand. He dips his head once. Then dives back into the waves. Keen watches him go.

cuts.framing: medium two-shot of Keen and Aida, 50mm eye-level, still and honest
cuts.action: Keen watches Squeaky go, then turns to Aida with a question. Aida smiles.
cuts.delivery: quiet curiosity with a small catch of worry underneath
cuts.voiceTreatment: voice forward, surf low

cuts.framing: intimate close two-shot, 85mm, locked; Aida steady, Keen absorbing
cuts.action: Aida smiles. Keen absorbs that. A soft grateful pause. Aida gives him the smallest nod.
cuts.dialogue: AIDA: Just getting a message home to your Mum. To let her know you’re okay.
cuts.delivery: Aida: warm, measured, practical tenderness; Keen: soft, grateful, almost a whisper after a held pause
cuts.voiceTreatment: Aida warm and close; pause before Keen’s line preserved; no overlap

beats.cameraArc: The camera turns from the welcome circle back to the open sea, then compresses distance through Aida’s gesture and settles into a locked close two-shot for the homeward reassurance.
beats.pauseHold: Hold 2 seconds after Aida’s explanation before Keen’s “Thank you.”, then hold 1.5 seconds on Aida’s smallest nod.

performance.surface: Keen simply asks what Aida did and thanks her.
performance.underneath: He feels the pull back to Mum, the relief that she will know, and the first trust that these new bears understand what home means.
performance.innerThought: Mum won’t be standing there wondering. Someone here thought of her too.

beats.crystalGlow: Aida: rose quartz steady-warm, not flashy; Keen: vacant wristbands faint steady with a tiny aquamarine ache, no gems; group crystals remain soft in background; cove crystals dim to a gentle pulse as Squeaky leaves.

beats.storyBeat: In the washed-clean gathering circle, Aida and Keen stand at the center while the Crystal Bears witness him; Aida names the courage he already showed, and the first aquamarine shimmer answers between them.
beats.emotionalIntent: The audience should feel the quiet shock of being truly seen, not praised for performance but recognised for the love-driven choice already made.
beats.want: Keen wants to stand steady and receive this moment like a brave, grown-up explorer.
beats.need: Keen needs to understand that he does not have to perform bravery for this family; they already saw the frightened, loving choice underneath.
beats.crystalTruth: Aida’s steady warmth and the first aquamarine shimmer reveal Keen’s need to be recognised, not rewarded; his face tries to stay composed, but the light brightens toward the truth that courage was already inside him.
beats.kidRead: The magical light starts because Aida says Keen did something brave.
beats.adultRead: A child who has been trying to prove himself is finally witnessed by safe adults without being made smaller.
beats.theGame: The Welcome Circle: the circle only lights when everyone quietly witnesses what was already true, not when someone boasts or wins.
beats.physicalFeeling: A warm bloom in the chest, like sunlight returning after holding your breath.
beats.light: Hopeful late-afternoon post-storm sun isolates the center of the circle; Aida and Keen sit in a soft warm pool while faint aquamarine reflections begin between them.
beats.atmosphere: Fresh droplets on leaves sparkle in still air; the cove feels washed clean, with tiny crystal motes gathering slowly toward the center.
beats.motionTempo: Slow, reverent, almost ceremonial: small breaths, tiny eye shifts, and one gentle shimmer instead of big movement.
beats.grade: Warm post-storm gold with a soft aquamarine core, preserving tenderness rather than spectacle.

cuts.framing: wide establishing, 24mm, low child-height, slow breathing push-in
cuts.action: The storm has passed. Everything glows fresh and washed clean. Droplets cling to leaves and sparkle in the sunlight. The Crystal Bears gather in a quiet circle. Aida and Keen stand at the center. Keen still wears his father’s wristbands. Aida looks at him with warmth and certainty.

cuts.framing: medium two-shot, 50mm, locked and honest at Aida and Keen’s eye-line
cuts.action: Aida holds her stillness and lets the circle’s attention rest on Keen without rushing him.
cuts.dialogue: AIDA: You followed your heart… and chose courage.
cuts.delivery: warm, certain, quiet enough that it feels like recognition rather than announcement
cuts.voiceTreatment: canonical Aida voice in post; soft forward presence

cuts.framing: close-up on Keen, 85mm, no camera movement
cuts.action: A gentle AQUAMARINE LIGHT begins to shimmer in the air between them. Keen takes it in, small and stunned, as the light begins to answer Aida’s words.

beats.cameraArc: Begin with the whole circle as a safe witness, then move inward until the frame belongs to the tiny space between Aida’s certainty and Keen’s wonder.
beats.pauseHold: Hold on Keen’s face for 2 seconds after the first shimmer begins, letting recognition land before magic escalates.

performance.surface: Aida is steady and warm; Keen tries to stand tall and composed in the middle of everyone.
performance.underneath: Aida is naming the truth she witnessed; Keen is surprised that the part of him he did not perform is the part everyone sees.
performance.innerThought: Keen: They saw me when I was scared, and they are still looking at me like I belong.

beats.atmosphere: The post-storm air is still and bright; droplets glitter softly as the circle closes and opens again, leaving warmth in the space around Keen.

beats.grade: Golden post-storm warmth with aquamarine highlights, preserving a bittersweet homecoming rather than a perfect reset.
